name: Aisha
observedShift: Aisha is in week four, remote, and has started driving planning threads, but she still looks uncertain in cross-functional meetings when priorities conflict. I need to understand what is still unclear around decision rights, partner expectations, and how to run alignment without over-indexing on consensus. She has good energy, but I do not want her spending it decoding unspoken norms. This meeting should surface the specific friction points and translate them into practical support and clearer boundaries.

focusPoints.label: Decision rights and expectations in cross-functional work.
focusPoints.reason: Whether the scope and decision rights are still fuzzy when priorities conflict, and what “good” looks like for her in those moments.

focusPoints.label: Running alignment without over-indexing on consensus.
focusPoints.reason: How she should handle partner expectations when tradeoffs are needed—what to push for, what to escalate, and when to decide rather than keep seeking agreement.

focusPoints.label: What support and boundaries she needs from you.
focusPoints.reason: What she would want more of from you right now so she stops spending energy decoding unspoken norms and can run planning threads with clearer guardrails.

focusPoints.label: How she’s landing with partners day to day.
focusPoints.reason: How she’s building trust and comfort with cross-functional partners so she can surface friction early without waiting for consensus to form.
